flashcards.front: Define a manifold in the context of dimensionality reduction.
flashcards.back: A manifold is a topological space that locally resembles Euclidean space. In ML, we assume high-dimensional data actually lies on a lower-dimensional curved surface embedded in higher-dimensional space. Manifold learning aims to discover this intrinsic low-dimensional structure.

flashcards.front: What is Isomap? Provide its key mathematical formulation.
flashcards.back: Isomap (Isometric Mapping) estimates geodesic distances on the manifold by computing shortest-path distances in a k-NN graph, then applies MDS to these distances. Formula: D_geo(i,j) ≈ graph_distance(i,j); final embedding minimizes ||τ(D_geo) - τ(D_embedding)|| where τ applies double centering for Gram matrix eigendecomposition.

flashcards.front: Explain Locally Linear Embedding (LLE) and its computational complexity.
flashcards.back: LLE assumes data lies on a manifold locally composed of linear patches. Step 1: Find k-nearest neighbors and compute reconstruction weights W minimizing ||x_i - Σ_j W_ij x_j||². Step 2: Compute embedding Y minimizing ||y_i - Σ_j W_ij y_j||². Complexity: O(DkN²) for weight computation, O(N³) for eigenvalue solving. For N samples, D dimensions, k neighbors.

Laplacian Eigenmaps constructs a weighted graph from data (using k-NN or ε-ball), computes the graph Laplacian L = D - W (unnormalized) or D^(-1/2)L D^(-1/2) (normalized), then embeds by solving Ly = λDy for smallest nonzero eigenvalues. Preserves local neighborhood structure; connected points stay close in embedding.

flashcards.front: Compare t-SNE with UMAP for visualization tasks.
flashcards.back: t-SNE uses t-distribution for Student noise in low-dim, preserves local structure well but not global; O(N²) complexity, stochastic, not deterministic. UMAP uses fuzzy simplicial sets and topological data analysis; preserves both local and global structure better; faster O(N log N); deterministic. t-SNE better for small datasets, UMAP scales better for large datasets.

flashcards.front: When should you use PCA vs Manifold Learning methods?
flashcards.back: Use PCA when data is linearly correlated (global structure matters) and for preprocessing/denoising; O(min(D³,N³)), interpretable. Use manifold learning when data lies on nonlinear manifolds (curved surfaces), for visualization of complex data, or when local structure preservation is crucial. PCA fails on Swiss roll data; Isomap/LLE succeed.

flashcards.front: What is the curse of dimensionality in manifold learning?
flashcards.back: As intrinsic dimension D increases, distance-based methods degrade because all points become equidistant (distance concentration). Also, to reliably estimate local manifold structure, need exponentially more samples. Manifold learning works best when intrinsic dimension << observed dimension and sufficient data exists.

flashcards.front: List practical applications of manifold learning in CS.
flashcards.back: 1) Image/video analysis (face recognition, gesture recognition); 2) NLP (word embedding visualization); 3) Biomedical signal processing (EEG, fMRI); 4) Sensor networks; 5) Molecular dynamics; 6) Anomaly detection; 7) Preprocessing for classification/regression when features are redundant.
